defect: Official outline and live manuscript state conflict over whether Chapter 12 is Europe/xAI/rest-of-frontier or Anthropic/Claude.
risk_to_book: The book can silently lose either the mandatory Anthropic/Claude spine or the mandatory Europe/xAI/rest-frontier coverage, and chapter numbering becomes unreliable.
fastest_repair: Make an explicit final structure decision before the Chapter 12 prose placement pass: retitle/split/relocate rather than letting two Chapter 12s coexist.

defect: Live filenames are one number behind the official outline after the Chapter 1 draft was added.
risk_to_book: Final layout, references, visual captions, and source cues can point to the wrong chapter even if prose is correct.
fastest_repair: Rename or final-alias early technical chapter files during a dedicated layout/renumbering pass.

defect: GTC 2026 roadmap/stage material sits beside physical-infrastructure constraints and can be misread as deployed-history evidence.
risk_to_book: Roadmap slides may become unsupported claims about shipped systems, facility performance, power solutions, or deployment scale.
fastest_repair: Keep Chapter 15 as attributed stagecraft and Chapter 16 as independent physical constraints; require announced/roadmap/shipped labels.

defect: Tools, code, and Claude Code form a good sequence but share agent-loop grammar and overlapping MCP/computer-use material.
risk_to_book: Readers may feel repetition instead of escalation from general tools to code to supervised repository work.
fastest_repair: Keep Chapter 18 general, Chapter 19 code-as-domain, and Chapter 20 terminal/repository case study with one selected Claude Code docs screenshot.

defect: Late book chronology is mostly thematic, not event-linear; this is acceptable only if each chapter states its source contract.
risk_to_book: The final third can feel like essays after the historical race unless handoff beats make inference, money, trust, and judgment one chain.
fastest_repair: Add one late-book tension pass that explicitly links reasoning cost, inference economics, failure/trust, and human judgment.

defect: Source and visual ledgers are stronger than exact chapter-date ledgers; many chapters have source rows but not a compact date-control row.
risk_to_book: Dated events may be true locally but hard to audit globally, especially for mutable product pages and benchmark snapshots.
fastest_repair: Use this table as the canonical chapter-date audit and update it after major prose/structure changes.
